input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Section 1: Dashboard (single page) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Section 2: Products (two sub-tabs) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings page in the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

Let me create the files for them.

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**`AppShell.xaml`** — Three `FlyoutItem` entries:

| Flyout entry | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Single page — `ShellContent` directly inside `FlyoutItem` |
| **Products** | A `<Tab>` with two `ShellContent` children → renders as bottom tabs ("Active" / "Archived") |
| **Settings** | Single page, same pattern as Dashboard |

**`AppShell.xaml.cs`** — Registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a programmatic route so you can navigate to it from either product list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    ["Product"] = selectedProduct
});
```

To receive the parameter in `ProductDetailsPage`, decorate it with `[QueryProperty]`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(Product), "Product")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public Product Product { get; set; }
}
```

**Key points:**
- `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` = lazy loading (pages created only when visited).
- Only pages **not** in the visual tree need `Routing.RegisterRoute`. The shell-declared pages already have implicit routes from their `Route` attributes.
- The `<Tab>` wrapper is what gives you the two bottom tabs under the Products flyout item.
